classRules.description: Combined use increases the risk of serotonin syndrome.
classRules.management: Avoid combination when possible. Seek urgent care if agitation, fever, or muscle rigidity occur.

classRules.description: SSRIs with MAOIs risk fatal serotonin syndrome.
classRules.management: Contraindicated. Requires long washout between classes.

classRules.description: SSRIs with NSAIDs increase bleeding risk.
classRules.management: Use lowest NSAID dose for shortest time; watch for bruising or GI bleeding.

classRules.description: Triptans with SSRIs may increase serotonin syndrome risk.
classRules.management: Use only under medical guidance; know serotonin syndrome symptoms.

classRules.description: SSRIs with beta blockers may affect heart rate and blood pressure in some patients.
classRules.management: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate when starting or changing doses.

classRules.description: Anticoagulants with NSAIDs increase serious bleeding risk.
classRules.management: Avoid NSAIDs unless specifically approved; use acetaminophen when appropriate.

classRules.description: Anticoagulant plus antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk.
classRules.management: Only combine when prescribed together; report any bleeding promptly.

classRules.description: NSAIDs may reduce blood pressure control and harm kidneys with ACE inhibitors.
classRules.management: Limit NSAID use; stay hydrated; monitor blood pressure.

classRules.description: NSAIDs may reduce ARB efficacy and worsen kidney function.
classRules.management: Limit NSAID use; monitor blood pressure and kidney labs.

classRules.description: Opioids with benzodiazepines increase sedation, respiratory depression, and overdose risk.
classRules.management: Avoid combining unless specifically prescribed with close monitoring.

classRules.description: Macrolides raise statin levels, increasing myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk.
classRules.management: Often requires holding or changing statin during antibiotic course.

classRules.description: Fluconazole greatly increases statin exposure and muscle toxicity risk.
classRules.management: Avoid combination or use lowest statin dose with monitoring.

classRules.description: Statin with fibrate significantly increases severe muscle injury risk.
classRules.management: Generally avoided; alternative lipid therapy may be needed.

classRules.description: Beta blockers with verapamil risk dangerous bradycardia and hypotension.
classRules.management: Avoid routine combination unless cardiology supervises.

classRules.description: Beta blockers with diltiazem can cause excessive heart rate slowing.
classRules.management: Use only with close cardiac monitoring.

classRules.description: Non-selective beta blockers can block bronchodilation and worsen asthma.
classRules.management: Generally avoid propranolol in asthma; cardioselective beta blockers may be used cautiously.

classRules.description: NSAIDs reduce lithium clearance and can cause toxicity.
classRules.management: Avoid NSAIDs when possible; check lithium levels if unavoidable.

classRules.description: ACE inhibitors can raise lithium levels.
classRules.management: Monitor lithium levels when starting or changing ACE inhibitors.

classRules.description: Some PPIs may reduce activation of clopidogrel in certain patients.
classRules.management: Discuss alternative PPI (e.g. pantoprazole) with your prescriber.

pairs.description: Combined use increases the risk of serotonin syndrome.
pairs.management: Avoid combination when possible. Seek urgent care if agitation, fever, or muscle rigidity occur.

pairs.description: Combined use increases the risk of serotonin syndrome.
pairs.management: Avoid combination when possible unless closely supervised.

pairs.description: Combined use increases the risk of serotonin syndrome.
pairs.management: Avoid combination when possible unless closely supervised.

pairs.description: Linezolid has MAOI activity and can cause serotonin syndrome with SSRIs.
pairs.management: Do not combine. Allow appropriate washout periods between drugs.

pairs.description: Linezolid has MAOI activity and can cause serotonin syndrome with SSRIs.
pairs.management: Do not combine. Allow appropriate washout periods between drugs.

pairs.description: SSRI with MAOI risks fatal serotonin syndrome.
pairs.management: Contraindicated. Requires long washout between classes.

pairs.description: SSRI with MAOI risks fatal serotonin syndrome.
pairs.management: Contraindicated. Requires long washout between classes.

pairs.description: SSRIs with NSAIDs increase bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Use lowest NSAID dose for shortest time; watch for bruising or GI bleeding.

pairs.description: SSRIs with NSAIDs increase bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Use lowest NSAID dose for shortest time; watch for bruising or GI bleeding.

pairs.description: SSRIs with antiplatelet agents increase bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Monitor for unusual bleeding; discuss GI protection with your clinician.

pairs.description: Triptans with SSRIs may increase serotonin syndrome risk.
pairs.management: Use only under medical guidance; know serotonin syndrome symptoms.

pairs.description: SSRIs with beta blockers may increase effects on heart rate and blood pressure in some patients.
pairs.management: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate, especially when starting or changing doses.

pairs.description: Fluoxetine can raise propranolol levels, increasing bradycardia and hypotension risk.
pairs.management: May need propranolol dose adjustment; monitor vitals.

pairs.description: Combination greatly increases bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Only combine when prescribed together; report any bleeding promptly.

pairs.description: NSAIDs with warfarin increase serious bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Avoid NSAIDs unless specifically approved; use acetaminophen when appropriate.

pairs.description: NSAIDs with warfarin increase serious bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Avoid NSAIDs unless specifically approved.

pairs.description: Fluconazole can markedly increase warfarin effect.
pairs.management: Frequent INR monitoring and warfarin dose changes often required.

pairs.description: Some antibiotics alter warfarin metabolism and INR.
pairs.management: Extra INR monitoring during and after antibiotic course.

pairs.description: Anticoagulant plus antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Only when prescribed dual therapy; report bleeding or dark stools.

pairs.description: Anticoagulant plus antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Only when prescribed dual therapy; report bleeding promptly.

pairs.description: Omeprazole may reduce activation of clopidogrel in some patients.
pairs.management: Discuss alternative PPI (e.g. pantoprazole) with your prescriber.

pairs.description: ACE inhibitors with potassium supplements can cause hyperkalemia.
pairs.management: Monitor potassium levels; avoid unsupervised potassium supplements.

pairs.description: NSAIDs may reduce blood pressure control and harm kidneys with ACE inhibitors.
pairs.management: Limit NSAID use; stay hydrated; monitor blood pressure.

pairs.description: NSAIDs may reduce ARB efficacy and worsen kidney function.
pairs.management: Limit NSAID use; monitor blood pressure and kidney labs.

pairs.description: Beta blockers with non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blockers risk severe bradycardia or heart block.
pairs.management: Avoid routine combination unless cardiology supervises.

pairs.description: Beta blockers with verapamil risk dangerous bradycardia and hypotension.
pairs.management: Avoid routine combination unless cardiology supervises.

pairs.description: Beta blockers with diltiazem can cause excessive heart rate slowing.
pairs.management: Use only with close cardiac monitoring.

pairs.description: Beta blockers may mask symptoms of low blood sugar.
pairs.management: Monitor glucose closely; know non-heart symptoms of hypoglycemia.

pairs.description: Beta blockers may blunt some signs of hypoglycemia.
pairs.management: Monitor blood sugar regularly if you have diabetes.

pairs.description: Macrolides raise statin levels, increasing myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk.
pairs.management: Often requires holding or changing statin during antibiotic course.

pairs.description: Clarithromycin increases statin exposure and muscle toxicity risk.
pairs.management: Discuss temporary statin changes with your prescriber.

pairs.description: Fluconazole greatly increases simvastatin levels.
pairs.management: Avoid combination or use lowest statin dose with monitoring.

pairs.description: Statin with fibrate significantly increases severe muscle injury risk.
pairs.management: Generally avoided; alternative lipid therapy may be needed.

pairs.description: Statin with fibrate significantly increases muscle toxicity risk.
pairs.management: Generally avoided unless specialist directs.

pairs.description: NSAIDs reduce lithium clearance and can cause toxicity.
pairs.management: Avoid NSAIDs when possible; check lithium levels if unavoidable.

pairs.description: ACE inhibitors can raise lithium levels.
pairs.management: Monitor lithium levels when starting or changing ACE inhibitors.

pairs.description: Alcohol with metformin increases lactic acidosis risk, especially with heavy use.
pairs.management: Limit alcohol; avoid binge drinking.

pairs.description: Disulfiram-like reaction: nausea, vomiting, flushing with alcohol.
pairs.management: Avoid alcohol during treatment and for 48–72 hours after the last dose.

pairs.description: Opioids with benzodiazepines increase sedation, respiratory depression, and overdose risk.
pairs.management: Avoid combining unless specifically prescribed with close monitoring.

pairs.description: Opioids with benzodiazepines increase sedation and respiratory depression.
pairs.management: Avoid combining unless specifically prescribed with close monitoring.

pairs.description: Opioids with benzodiazepines increase overdose risk.
pairs.management: Avoid combining unless specifically prescribed.

pairs.description: Tramadol with benzodiazepines increases CNS and respiratory depression.
pairs.management: Avoid combination when possible.

pairs.description: Sedative combinations increase confusion, falls, and respiratory depression.
pairs.management: Avoid using multiple sedatives together.

pairs.description: Combined CNS depressants increase sedation and fall risk.
pairs.management: Use caution; avoid driving until you know the effect.

pairs.description: Combined CNS depressants increase sedation.
pairs.management: Start low, go slow; avoid alcohol.

pairs.description: PPIs may reduce levothyroxine absorption.
pairs.management: Separate dosing times; recheck thyroid labs after PPI changes.

pairs.description: Calcium can bind levothyroxine and lower absorption.
pairs.management: Take levothyroxine on empty stomach; separate calcium by several hours.

pairs.description: Iron reduces levothyroxine absorption.
pairs.management: Separate doses by at least 4 hours.

pairs.description: Amlodipine can increase simvastatin levels.
pairs.management: Simvastatin dose often limited to 20 mg with amlodipine.

pairs.description: Amiodarone raises digoxin levels and toxicity risk.
pairs.management: Digoxin dose reduction and level monitoring usually required.

pairs.description: Verapamil increases digoxin concentration.
pairs.management: Monitor digoxin levels and heart rhythm.

pairs.description: Non-selective beta blockers can block bronchodilation and worsen asthma.
pairs.management: Generally avoid propranolol in asthma; cardioselective beta blockers may be used cautiously.

pairs.description: Bupropion with SSRIs increases seizure and serotonin syndrome risk.
pairs.management: Use only when benefits outweigh risks; monitor closely.

pairs.description: Lowers seizure threshold and adds serotonin effect.
pairs.management: Use caution; report agitation or tremor promptly.

pairs.description: NSAIDs can reduce methotrexate clearance and cause toxicity.
pairs.management: Often contraindicated; discuss all pain relievers with your prescriber.

pairs.description: Steroids plus NSAIDs increase GI ulcer and bleeding risk.
pairs.management: Use GI protection if combination is necessary.

pairs.description: Combined RAAS agents increase hyperkalemia risk.
pairs.management: Monitor potassium and kidney function.

pairs.description: Spironolactone with potassium supplements risks dangerous hyperkalemia.
pairs.management: Avoid routine potassium supplements unless directed and monitored.

pairs.description: Calcium and antacids reduce fluoroquinolone absorption.
pairs.management: Separate doses by at least 2 hours before or 6 hours after.

pairs.description: Calcium reduces tetracycline absorption.
pairs.management: Separate doses by several hours.

pairs.description: Beta blockers can blunt epinephrine response and cause severe hypertension if unopposed alpha stimulation occurs.
pairs.management: Inform clinicians and dentists that you take a beta blocker.
